title: Variables and expressions
goalText: Treat chute choice as data your program can store and reuse.
machineObjectiveText: Read the active portal side and send a ball into that portal on purpose.
boardMeaningText: portal_side tells you which side portal is active right now: 1 = left, 3 = right.
nextActionText: Read portal_side, pass it into choose_chute(...), and trigger a portal split. Each successful run adds one progress step.
unlockSpotlightText: Portal control is unlocked. You can now read portal_side, store it in a variable, and send that value into choose_chute(...).

title: Conditionals
goalText: React to the next ball before it launches.
machineObjectiveText: Use if to skip evil balls and keep the good ones.
boardMeaningText: next_ball previews the next ball in line. Evil balls should be skipped. Lucky balls are worth extra points.
nextActionText: Use if next_ball == evil_ball to decide when to skip_ball(). A run that skips at least one evil ball adds one progress step.
unlockSpotlightText: Ball preview is unlocked. You can now read next_ball, compare it to ball-type constants, and skip bad balls before they launch.

title: Functions
goalText: Store one reusable tactic and call it when you need it.
machineObjectiveText: Use a helper function to launch visibly marked helper shots.
boardMeaningText: Helper calls now create subtly marked shots that still use the same chutes, portals, ball types, and bucket scores.
nextActionText: Call your helper during a good run. If a helper-marked ball causes a portal split or lands as a lucky score, the run adds one progress step.
unlockSpotlightText: Helper shots are unlocked. Helper calls now send visibly marked balls through the same chutes, portals, ball types, and bucket scores.

title: Loops
goalText: Repeat one good tactic on purpose instead of rewriting it by hand.
machineObjectiveText: Use a for-loop to launch a short multi-ball plan.
boardMeaningText: Loops repeat the same tactic several times in one run. continue can skip the rest of the current loop step after an evil ball is skipped.
nextActionText: Wrap a good tactic in for ball in range(n):. Use continue after skip_ball() when you want that loop step to stop there. A run that launches at least 3 balls and earns 2 positive outcomes adds one progress step.
unlockSpotlightText: Repeated shots are unlocked. for-loops can now feed the machine several times in one run, and continue can skip the rest of one loop step.
